title: jarava hypsophila
intro: Jarava hypsophila, also known as Queñua, is a species of grass native to the Andean regions of South America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Jarava hypsophila include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and treat respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction by indigenous communities in the Andean region, who value its medicinal and nutritional properties.
